article_title: Starcloud Reaches Unicorn Status With $170 Million Series A to Build Data Centers in Orbit as Terrestrial AI Infrastructure Hits Its Limits

content_preview.title: Starcloud Reaches Unicorn Status With $170 Million Series A to Build Data Centers in Orbit as Terrestrial AI Infrastructure Hits Its Limits
content_preview.summary: The Y Combinator graduate plans an 88,000-satellite constellation for orbital AI compute, backed by Benchmark and EQT Ventures.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Starcloud, a Redmond, Washington-based startup building data centers in orbit, has raised $170 million in a Series A round that values the company at $1.1 billion, according to [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/30/starcloud-raises-170-million-series-ato-build-data-centers-in-space/). The round, led by Benchmark and EQT Ventures, makes Starcloud a unicorn just 17 months after its Y Combinator demo day presentation, one of the fastest paths to that milestone among YC alumni....
